branding_strategy.name: string (display name)
branding_strategy.description: string (what it is, why it matters)
branding_strategy.validation_score: number (1-10, how much evidence it generates)
branding_strategy.audience_fit: number (1-10, how well it targets your audience)
branding_strategy.speed_to_evidence: number (1-10, how fast you get real data)
branding_strategy.brand_clarity_boost: number (0-100, points added to brand score)
branding_strategy.why_score: string (one-line reason for the validation/audience/speed scores)

marketing_experiment.name: string (display name)
marketing_experiment.description: string (what it is, why it matters)
marketing_experiment.validation_score: number (1-10, how much evidence it generates)
marketing_experiment.audience_fit: number (1-10, how well it targets your audience)
marketing_experiment.speed_to_evidence: number (1-10, how fast you get real data)
marketing_experiment.required_brand_score: number (0-100, minimum brand score to unlock this experiment)
brand_strategy_tags: string (tags matching branding strategies it depends on)
marketing_experiment.why_score: string (one-line reason for the validation/audience/speed scores)

branding_strategies.name: Brand Positioning Workshop
branding_strategies.description: Define your unique value proposition and how you differ from competitors. Run a 2-hour workshop with your team (or yourself) to articulate: what you do, who it's for, and why it's different.
branding_strategies.why_score: Forces you to articulate your value proposition in one sentence; clarity is the fastest form of validation.

branding_strategies.name: Customer Persona Deep-Dive
branding_strategies.description: Interview 10-15 potential users to build detailed personas. Ask about their day, pain points, and current solutions. Record and transcribe for quotes.
branding_strategies.why_score: Direct conversations with real humans are the single highest-validation activity; personas make every future experiment sharper.

branding_strategies.name: Messaging Framework Sprint
branding_strategies.description: Craft consistent taglines, elevator pitches, and key messages. Write 3 versions of your core message and test them on 5 people for comprehension.
branding_strategies.why_score: If people can't repeat your pitch back to you, your ads won't work either.

branding_strategies.name: Visual Identity Audit & Refresh
branding_strategies.description: Review your logo, colors, and typography. Create a simple one-page style guide. If you have nothing, use a free tool like Canva to make a basic logo and color palette.
branding_strategies.why_score: Visual trust signals matter for conversion, but this is lower-priority validation than messaging clarity.

branding_strategies.name: Competitor Differentiation Map
branding_strategies.description: Map 5-8 competitors on a 2x2 grid (e.g., price vs. convenience). Identify the empty quadrant you occupy. This becomes your positioning anchor.
branding_strategies.why_score: Knowing where you sit relative to others prevents you from accidentally copying an incumbent.

branding_strategies.name: Value Proposition Canvas
branding_strategies.description: Use the Strategyzer canvas to map customer jobs, pains, and gains against your products/services, pain relievers, and gain creators. Fill both sides in one session.
branding_strategies.why_score: Fastest way to check if your product actually solves a real problem; one day of honest work prevents weeks of wasted marketing.

branding_strategies.name: Brand Voice Guidelines
branding_strategies.description: Define tone, voice, and writing style. Write a 1-page guide: are you casual or formal? Witty or serious? Consistent voice builds trust across channels.
branding_strategies.why_score: Inconsistent messaging confuses users; a voice guide makes every tweet, email, and ad sound like the same person wrote it.

branding_strategies.name: Landing Page Copy Rewrite
branding_strategies.description: Rewrite your landing page around a single, clear value proposition. One headline, one subhead, one CTA. Remove everything else. Test comprehension on 3 strangers.
branding_strategies.why_score: Your landing page is your most-visited brand asset; if the copy is vague, every ad dollar is wasted.

marketing_experiments.name: Campus Interviews
marketing_experiments.description: Go to campus and interview 20-30 students about the problem you solve. Ask open-ended questions, don't pitch. Record quotes that validate or invalidate your hypothesis.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Direct conversations with real students give the clearest signal about whether the problem is real, at almost no cost — before spending anything on ads.

marketing_experiments.name: Instagram Ad Test
marketing_experiments.description: Run a $5/day Instagram ad for 4 days targeting your audience demographic. Measure CTR and landing page conversion. Useful once initial interest is confirmed.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Useful once initial interest is confirmed, but on its own it only tells you people clicked — not that they actually want the product.

marketing_experiments.name: Landing Page A/B Test
marketing_experiments.description: Build two versions of your landing page with different headlines or CTAs. Split traffic 50/50 and measure which converts better. Requires a clear value proposition first.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Optimizes conversion but requires a strong baseline message; testing weak copy just tells you which version of 'unclear' is slightly less bad.

marketing_experiments.name: Referral Program
marketing_experiments.description: Incentivize early users to refer friends with a simple reward (e.g., $10 credit for both sides). Track referral rate and cost per acquisition.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Only works if people already love your product; a referral program for a product nobody wants is just giving away money.

marketing_experiments.name: Targeted Cold Outreach
marketing_experiments.description: Send 50 highly personalized emails or DMs to prospects who match your persona. Reference something specific about them. Track reply rate and meeting booking rate.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Zero cost, high signal — if you can't get 5 replies out of 50 personalized messages, your value proposition isn't compelling enough yet.

marketing_experiments.name: Content Marketing Sprint
marketing_experiments.description: Write 3-5 blog posts, Twitter threads, or LinkedIn posts that solve problems for your audience. Don't sell — teach. Track engagement and inbound inquiries.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Builds long-term trust and SEO, but takes 2-4 weeks to see meaningful data; not for founders who need answers in 3 days.

marketing_experiments.name: Reddit/Forum Engagement
marketing_experiments.description: Answer questions in relevant subreddits or forums for 3 days. Be genuinely helpful, mention your solution only when it directly answers the question. Track upvotes and DMs.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Reddit communities are brutally honest — if your solution is genuinely useful, you'll know fast. If it's not, you'll know even faster.

marketing_experiments.name: Beta Waitlist Campaign
marketing_experiments.description: Build a simple waitlist landing page and promote it across your channels. Measure sign-up rate and email open rate. Gauge real demand before building anything.
marketing_experiments.why_score: A waitlist with 0 sign-ups is devastating but honest — it tells you people don't care before you spend months building.

marketing_experiments.name: Influencer Micro-Partnership
marketing_experiments.description: Partner with a micro-influencer (1K-10K followers) in your niche for a sponsored post or story. Track clicks, sign-ups, and cost per acquisition.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Micro-influencers have high trust but small reach; only worth it if your visual identity and messaging are polished enough to convert their audience.

marketing_experiments.name: Ambassador Program
marketing_experiments.description: Recruit 5-10 passionate early users as brand ambassadors. Give them early access, swag, or equity. Track their referrals and social mentions.
marketing_experiments.why_score: Highest brand score requirement because ambassadors need a clear, compelling story to tell. If your positioning is fuzzy, they won't know what to say.
